paper_evidence.summary: In ANBL6 multiple myeloma cells, transfection with an activating N-ras12 construct protects cells from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis; protection is comparable to that achieved in parental cells with high IL-6 concentrations, indicating that activated N-ras confers resistance to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
paper_evidence.location: Abstract; Introduction/Results; Figure 5 (protection from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis correlates with N-ras/K-ras activation).
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Figure 5 shows reduced percentage of apoptotic nuclei over 4 days in Nras12 cells versus parental pLXSN cells across IL-6 doses; high IL-6 (10 ng/ml) approximates the protection seen in Nras12/Kras12 lines.

fields.evidence_description: NRAS codon 12 mutations confer resistance to dexamethasone in multiple myeloma cell lines. ANBL6 cells transfected with N-ras12 gene were protected from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis, with protection levels comparable to cells treated with high concentrations (10 ng/ml) of IL-6.
fields.verbatim_quote: Interestingly, the ANBL6 cell line transfected with an N-ras12 or a K-ras12 gene is protected from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

ai_items.notes: Accurately captures that activated N-ras (codon 12) protects ANBL6 myeloma cells from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis. The term 'resistance in multiple myeloma cell lines' is a reasonable D-level summary but slightly extrapolates toward clinical resistance.

paper_evidence.summary: ANBL6 cells transfected with an activating K-ras12 construct are protected from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis, similar to N-ras12 cells and to parental cells treated with high IL-6, indicating that activated K-ras confers resistance to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Figure 5 line graph shows low apoptotic fraction over time in Kras12 cells relative to parental cells without high IL-6; protection increases with IL-6 concentration in parental cells.

fields.evidence_description: KRAS codon 12 mutations confer resistance to dexamethasone in multiple myeloma cell lines. ANBL6 cells transfected with K-ras12 gene were protected from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis, with protection levels comparable to cells treated with high concentrations (10 ng/ml) of IL-6.
fields.verbatim_quote: Interestingly, the ANBL6 cell line transfected with an N-ras12 or a K-ras12 gene is protected from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

ai_items.notes: Same pattern as NRAS: correctly encodes K-ras12-mediated protection from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis. The interpretation as 'resistance' is appropriate for CIViC D-level preclinical evidence.

paper_evidence.summary: In ANBL6.pLXSN parental cells, IL-6 enhances doxorubicin-induced apoptosis. In contrast, ANBL6.Nras12.EE cells, with high activated N-ras, are resistant to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis, indicating that activated N-ras confers resistance to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis in this myeloma model.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Figure 6 shows higher % apoptotic nuclei in IL-6-treated parental cells with doxorubicin versus much lower apoptosis in Nras12 and Kras12 cells over 4 days.

fields.evidence_description: NRAS codon 12 mutations confer resistance to doxorubicin in multiple myeloma cell lines. ANBL6 cells expressing N-ras12 were resistant to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis, in contrast to parental cells where IL-6 enhanced doxorubicin-induced apoptosis.
fields.verbatim_quote: Interestingly, the ANBL6.Nras12.EE and ANBL6.Kras12.EE cell lines, which have high levels of activated Ras, were resistant to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis.

ai_items.notes: Very faithful to the text and Figure 6: clearly states that N-ras12 cells are resistant to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis while parental IL-6-dependent cells undergo enhanced apoptosis.

paper_evidence.summary: ANBL6.Kras12.EE cells, which express activated K-ras, are resistant to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis, whereas IL-6 treatment of parental ANBL6 cells enhances doxorubicin-induced apoptosis.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Figure 6 line graph shows low apoptosis in Kras12 cells compared to IL-6-treated parental cells with 250 nM doxorubicin over several days.

fields.evidence_description: KRAS codon 12 mutations confer resistance to doxorubicin in multiple myeloma cell lines. ANBL6 cells expressing K-ras12 were resistant to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis, in contrast to parental cells where IL-6 enhanced doxorubicin-induced apoptosis.
fields.verbatim_quote: Interestingly, the ANBL6.Nras12.EE and ANBL6.Kras12.EE cell lines, which have high levels of activated Ras, were resistant to doxorubicin-induced apoptosis.

ai_items.notes: Correctly encodes K-ras12-mediated resistance to doxorubicin. This is one of the key actionable findings of the paper and is missing entirely from the ground truth.

paper_evidence.summary: The introduction/discussion notes that patients with high IL-6 expression or a Ras mutation have a worse prognosis in multiple myeloma, citing earlier clinical studies (Bataille et al., 1989; Liu et al., 1996). The statement is generic to Ras mutations rather than NRAS specifically.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: No survival curves or statistics are presented in this paper; the statement is supported only by external citations.
paper_evidence.evidence_level: D (if attached to this mechanistic paper alone)

fields.evidence_description: NRAS mutations are associated with worse prognosis in multiple myeloma patients. The study references that patients with Ras mutations (collectively including both NRAS and KRAS, which occur at 40-50% frequency in myeloma) have worse prognosis, potentially due to decreased sensitivity to therapeutic agents. The paper does not distinguish NRAS-specific prognostic effects from KRAS.
fields.verbatim_quote: Consistent with this, patients with high levels of IL-6 expression, or patients with a Ras mutation have a worse prognosis. Ras may be due in part to a decreased sensitivity to therapeutic agents.

ai_items.notes: Grounded in a real sentence but the underlying prognostic data come from prior clinical studies. Splitting the generic 'Ras mutation' statement into an NRAS-specific item is a modelling decision rather than something explicitly supported by this paper.

paper_evidence.summary: Same generic statement: patients with Ras mutations have worse prognosis in multiple myeloma, based on earlier cited clinical studies; not separated into NRAS vs KRAS in this paper.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: No new patient-level outcome data; statement based on previous work.
paper_evidence.evidence_level: D (for this paper as a source)

fields.evidence_description: KRAS mutations are associated with worse prognosis in multiple myeloma patients. The study references that patients with Ras mutations (collectively including both NRAS and KRAS, which occur at 40-50% frequency in myeloma) have worse prognosis, potentially due to decreased sensitivity to therapeutic agents. The paper does not distinguish KRAS-specific prognostic effects from NRAS.
fields.verbatim_quote: Consistent with this, patients with high levels of IL-6 expression, or patients with a Ras mutation have a worse prognosis. Ras may be due in part to a decreased sensitivity to therapeutic agents.

ai_items.notes: Same issues as the NRAS prognostic item: textually supported but based on external clinical data and not isoform-specific in the paper.

paper_evidence.summary: The Introduction notes that Ras mutations have been shown previously (Rowley et al., 2000) to protect myeloma cell lines from apoptosis induced by dexamethasone, doxorubicin, or melphalan. This paper does not present new melphalan experiments or specific KRAS G12X variants.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: No experimental data or statistics for melphalan in this paper; melphalan findings are entirely from the cited earlier study.
paper_evidence.evidence_type: PREDICTIVE (only if one treats the cited previous work as evidence here)

fields.evidence_description: In in vitro experiments, myeloma cell lines expressing activating NRAS and KRAS mutations are less sensitive to the apoptosis-inducing effects of dexamethasone, doxorubicin, and melphalan in comparison to cells with wildtype RAS.
fields.source_title: Activation of N-ras and K-ras induced by interleukin-6 in a myeloma cell line: implications for disease progression and therapeutic response.

gt_items.notes: Assigns a specific KRAS G12D substitution and melphalan as the curated therapy for this citation, although neither G12D nor melphalan experiments are actually presented in this paper.

fields.source_title: Activation of N-ras and K-ras induced by interleukin-6 in a myeloma cell line: implications for disease progression and therapeutic response.

gt_items.notes: Same mis-specification for KRAS G12C; no explicit variant or melphalan data in this paper.

fields.source_title: Activation of N-ras and K-ras induced by interleukin-6 in a myeloma cell line: implications for disease progression and therapeutic response.

gt_items.notes: Again, variant and therapy details go beyond what is supported by this paper.

fields.source_title: Activation of N-ras and K-ras induced by interleukin-6 in a myeloma cell line: implications for disease progression and therapeutic response.

gt_items.notes: All four GT items appear to be extrapolated from prior work (Rowley 2000), not from new melphalan experiments described in this paper.

missing_from_ai: No AI evidence item explicitly represents the generic 'Ras mutation (any isoform) associated with worse prognosis' as a single Ras-family PROGNOSTIC item; instead, it is split into separate NRAS and KRAS items. This is more of a modelling choice than a true omission.

No AI item encodes melphalan resistance based solely on the quoted prior work (Rowley et al., 2000). Given that this paper does not present new melphalan experiments, omitting this may actually be preferable for strict source fidelity.
ai_hallucinated_items: None of the six AI evidence items are fully unsupported by the paper. The two PROGNOSTIC items slightly over-interpret a generic 'Ras mutation' prognosis statement into isoform-specific NRAS and KRAS entries, but they clearly acknowledge this limitation in the descriptions.
ai_improvements_over_gt: Correctly captures dexamethasone and doxorubicin resistance mediated by NRAS and KRAS codon 12 mutations in ANBL6 cells, which are major experimental results in this paper but absent from the ground truth.
Uses generic 'CODON 12 MUTATION' rather than hallucinating specific KRAS G12D/C/A/S variants that are not reported in the paper.
Includes both NRAS and KRAS for predictive evidence, whereas ground truth only curates KRAS.
Adds Ras-mutation PROGNOSTIC items based on the explicit 'worse prognosis' sentence, which the ground truth does not represent at all.
Provides structured identifiers (Entrez IDs, EFO disease ID, NCIt and RxNorm therapy IDs) and variant_origin annotations, which are missing from ground truth and align with external ontologies.

overall_judgement.summary: For PMID 12483530, the AI extraction is substantially closer to what the paper actually does than the current CIViC-style ground truth. It correctly focuses on dexamethasone and doxorubicin resistance mediated by NRAS and KRAS codon 12 mutations in a multiple myeloma cell line and augments the record with reasonable Ras-related prognostic items and rich identifiers. The ground truth appears to over-attribute melphalan resistance and over-specify KRAS G12X variants that are not reported in this paper, while missing the core dexamethasone and doxorubicin findings.
strengths: Accurate capture of all four core predictive Ras–drug relationships demonstrated experimentally in this paper.
No clear hallucinations; verbatim quotes and evidence descriptions align closely with the text and figure summaries.
Richer and more conservative variant representation (codon 12) compared with ground truth's speculative G12X substitutions.
Addition of Ras-prognostic context and ontology IDs, improving clinical interpretability and machine-readability.
weaknesses: Prognostic items for NRAS and KRAS are based on an aggregated 'Ras mutation' statement from prior clinical literature; isoform-specific split is a modelling choice and should be clearly flagged or optionally collapsed into a generic Ras entry.
Evidence_level D for the prognostic items may not perfectly reflect the nature of the underlying external clinical data.

The extraction's self-critique claims 'no missing items'; depending on your curation policy, you may or may not want to include melphalan resistance (Rowley 2000) as an additional evidence item linked to this paper.
